asia.title: US launches more Iran strikes, hits oil tanker trying to break Hormuz blockade
asia.summary: The United States launched a new wave of strikes against Iran on Wednesday to curb its ability to threaten shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, as US President Donald Trump warned he could widen attacks unless the Islamic republic returned to talks. US Central Command said the strikes began at 1900 GMT and targeted Iranian military capabilities used to threaten vessels freely transiting the vital waterway. The US military also said one of its aircraft fired on and disabled an empty oil tanker that.

asia.title: 14-nation South China Sea statement is an expansionist overreach
asia.summary: On July 12, a coalition of 14 nations – the United States, Philippines, Australia, Canada, Britain, Japan, New Zealand, and seven European states including Germany, Italy and the Baltic nations – issued a statement marking the 10th anniversary of the South China Sea ruling at The Hague in 2016. The document recycled familiar arguments, urging compliance with the ruling and framing it as a cornerstone of the rules-based international order. But beneath it lies a fundamental disconnect from...

asia.title: Trump defence chief Hegseth unveils plan to test US troops for low testosterone
asia.summary: US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth announced on Wednesday that he is rolling out a new screening programme for “testosterone deficiency” among troops, calling it necessary to allow them to operate at their “absolute best”. The screenings will be conducted annually as part of service members’ required medical screenings for those 30 and older, he said. Troops under 30 can volunteer to be tested. In a video on social media, Hegseth said receiving testosterone replacement therapy would be...

briefing: Global tensions escalated as the US launched fresh strikes on Iran, with President Trump warning Tehran to "behave", while in the tech sphere, the US is strengthening its artificial intelligence posture towards China, with lawmakers advancing new export control bills. In Singapore, thousands of entry-level jobs for professionals, managers, executives, and technicians are available, with 32,800 vacancies reported in March, and GovTech is undergoing restructuring, affecting 7 to 9 percent of its workforce roles over the next two years. Meanwhile, the Straits Times Index and other regional markets are likely to be influenced by these global developments, as well as the ongoing US-China trade tensions and the narrowing trade gap in China.

digests.singapore: The Singapore job market is experiencing a mismatch between available entry-level positions for professionals, managers, executives, and technicians, and the challenges faced by fresh graduates in securing employment. Meanwhile, the government and businesses are undertaking initiatives to enhance inclusivity in public spaces and restructure workforce roles to focus on technical and outcome-driven skills, as seen in GovTech's restructuring plans. The island's social and economic landscape is also being shaped by concerns over drug consumption, transportation management, and the evolution of tourist attractions like Sentosa.
digests.us: The US is experiencing heightened tensions with Iran, with President Trump's actions potentially reigniting a "vicious cycle" of conflict, while a new poll suggests nearly half of Americans believe the war will last a year or more. Meanwhile, domestic politics are also contentious, with Senate Democrats grilling Trump's health nominees and a federal judge ruling that a Biden-era internet grant program unconstitutionally prioritized racial minorities. Additionally, Senator John Fetterman has warned that he would consider leaving the Democratic Party if it abandons its support for Israel.
digests.asia: The US has escalated its military actions against Iran, launching strikes to curb its ability to threaten shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, while also hardening its stance on China's artificial intelligence capabilities. In Southeast Asia, governments are addressing domestic issues, such as Thailand's development of a disaster insurance scheme and Malaysia's upcoming Negeri Sembilan election, which poses a challenge for Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim. Meanwhile, a 14-nation coalition has issued a statement on the South China Sea, reiterating the importance of complying with the 2016 ruling, amidst ongoing geopolitical tensions in the region.
digests.others: Tensions between the US and Iran have escalated with the US launching fresh strikes and Iran declaring a peace deal void, while in the Middle East, Russian attacks on Ukraine have resulted in civilian casualties. Meanwhile, a Pew study suggests that global confidence in China's leader Xi Jinping has surpassed that of US President Donald Trump, indicating a shift in international perceptions. Argentina's World Cup win over England has also been marked by political undertones, with the team celebrating with a banner referencing the Falkland Islands conflict.
digests.tech: Recent technology headlines highlight significant developments in pricing and innovation, with HP offering a discounted OLED-equipped laptop and Apple increasing the cost of AppleCare Plus subscriptions for Macs and iPads. Meanwhile, companies like Valve and Sheetz are making strategic decisions, such as reversing the discontinuation of Steam Deck batteries and migrating away from VMware due to uncertainty created by Broadcom. The intersection of technology and society is also a focus, with lawsuits over AI-generated content and discussions on the role of AI in design and production processes.
digests.economist: The Economist's analysis highlights the challenges faced by global leaders, including Donald Trump's limited options in the Gulf stand-off and the difficulties of making AI safe without relying on America and China. Meanwhile, businesses such as Eli Lilly are innovating by adopting preventive medicines and learning from big tech, while China's economy is experiencing a narrowing trade gap and stumbling into fiscal austerity. The global economy is also facing a fuel crunch due to rising oil prices, exacerbated by Trump's brinkmanship in the Hormuz region.
